data.title: JAPAN JAN TOTAL CPI +4.0% Y/Y, 41ST STRAIGHT RISE (DEC +3.6%); MEDIAN FORECAST +3.9%
Harmoney Price Target Raised 0.8% to A$1.30/Share by Forsyth Barr
Financials Down Amid Market Volatility — Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions gave back some recent gains as a wave of volatility washed through global markets.

Lloyds Banking Group put a further $881 million, or 700 million British pounds sterling, aside for a potential hit from a regulatory probe into historical commission practices in the U.K. auto-lending market, where it has long been a leader. The British bank's underlying earnings allayed investors' concerns about the implications of its writedowns, however.

A grand jury is investigating criminal misconduct at financial-technology firm Synapse, where customer funds went missing, and has questioned an executive who raised alarms before the company collapsed.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Savers Value Village Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Savers Value Village Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 15 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, identical to the same quarter last year. The mean expectation of eight analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 13 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 11 cents to 13 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 5% to $401.99 million from a year ago; analysts expected $401.25 million.

  • Savers Value Village Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 1 cent​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $1.9 million.

  • Savers Value Village Inc shares had risen by 9.3% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 1% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 5 "strong buy" or "buy," 3 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the miscellaneous specialty retailers peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Savers Value Village Inc is $13.50

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data February 20 at 11:32 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Century Aluminum Co reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Century Aluminum Co reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 49 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 39 cents. The mean expectation of three analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 45 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 33 cents to 56 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 23.2% to $631.00 million from a year ago; analysts expected $515.27 million.

  • Century Aluminum Co's reported EPS for the quarter was 47 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $45.2 million.

  • Century Aluminum Co shares had risen by 14.4% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 6% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the aluminum peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Century Aluminum Co is $24.00

data.title: SPY Raked In $4B Wednesday, Retakes Top Spot From VOO
data.description:
The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) pulled in more than $4 billion in new assets Wednesday as the S&P 500 index closed at a record-high for a second straight day, bringing the exchange-traded fund's total assets under management to $636 billion, according to etf.com daily fund flows data. 

The influx of capital boosted the former No.1 ETF by assets under management back to its perch atop the ETF hill, after losing its position earlier in the week to Vanguard’s S&P 500 ETF (VOO).

Among other top inflows, the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) attracted $2.6 billion, while the SPDR Gold Shares (GLD) added $756 million. The ProShares Ultra S&P 500 (SSO) saw inflows of $269 million, representing a 4.4% increase in assets. 

On the outflows side, the Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV) experienced outflows of $628.8 million, while the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY) saw $452.7 million exit the fund. The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) recorded outflows of $371 million. 

U.S. equity funds led all categories with over $7.9 billion in new assets, while commodity ETFs added $716 million. International equity funds pulled in $1.4 billion. Meanwhile, leveraged ETFs saw outflows of $131 million and all ETFs recorded total net inflows of $11.9 billion for the day.

Seatrium Proposes Final Dividend of S$0.015 a Share >5E2.SG
Air New Zealand Price Target Raised 8.5% to NZ$0.64/Share by Forsyth Barr
Rio Tinto Group Urges Shareholders to Vote Against Resolution to Abandon London Listing
data.description:
Rio Tinto Group urged its shareholders to vote against a proposal to abandon its primary listing in London and consolidate its shares on the Australian Securities Exchange, Financial Times reported Thursday.

The mining giant said such a move would be too costly and was not in the group's "best interests".

Activist investor Palliser Capital and others brought the resolution, arguing that a single Australian-domiciled holding company with a primary listing on the ASX would unlock $50 billion in shareholder value.

The group will hold its annual general meetings in London and Perth, Western Australia in April and May.

data.title: Seatrium: Revenue Primarily Driven by Strong Project Execution, Higher Business Activity >5E2.SG
Seatrium Capitalized on Industry Tailwinds to Secure New Projects in 2024, CEO Says
Tech Down on Resilience Bets — Tech Roundup
data.description:
Shares of technology companies fell, but not by as much as the broad market, as traders bet the sector would be less vulnerable than others to tariff fallout.

Alibaba Group's third-quarter revenue grew at its quickest pace since late 2023 as the e-commerce giant works to put growth back on track amid fierce domestic competition and a slowing Chinese economy.

Ryan Cohen, the investor and executive known for his work with Chewy.com and GameStop, is accumulating a substantial position in Alibaba.

Chinese tech firm Lenovo Group, the world's largest PC maker, plans to leverage AI startup DeepSeek's recent breakthrough to propel growth for its AI-enabled products.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Rural Funds Group Posts Lower Fiscal H1 Earnings, Higher Revenue
data.description:
Rural Funds Group reported Friday fiscal first-half diluted earnings of AU$0.0311 per stapled unit, down from AU$0.1133 per diluted share a year earlier.

Analysts polled by Visible Alpha expected earnings per unit of AU$0.091.

Adjusted funds from operations (AFFO) was AU$0.057, higher than AU$0.04 AFFO year on year. Analysts' consensus as compiled by Visible Alpha was expecting AU$0.055 AFFO.

Total revenue for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$62.2 million, compared with AU$53.2 million a year earlier. Analysts surveyed by Visible Alpha expected property revenue of AU$43.5 million.

Analysts polled by Visible Alpha expect net earnings of AU$0.10 per unit on property revenue of AU$47.5 million for the fiscal second half. Analysts polled by Visible Alpha expect net earnings of AU$0.191 per unit on property revenue of AU$95.6 million for full fiscal year.

The board declared a quarterly dividend of AU$0.029325 unchanged from AU$0.029325 in the previously reported period, paid Jan. 31 to shareholders on record as of Dec. 31, 2024.

SkyCity Entertainment Price Target Cut 6% to NZ$1.60/Share by Macquarie
Consumer Cos Down After Walmart Outlook — Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies fell after a disappointing outlook from one ubiquitous American discount chain.

Walmart shares slid as the world's largest bricks-and-mortar retailer set 2026 fiscal-year revenue and profit targets below analysts' expectations.

Toymaker Hasbro forecast modest sales growth in 2025, anticipating complications from a looming trade war.

Carrefour's stock fell after the company said it anticipated subdued earnings growth in 2025 as the grocer focuses on lowering prices to attract budget-squeezed customers.

German shoe brand Birkenstock reported higher revenue for its fiscal first quarter, helped by strong demand during the key holiday season, and said it continues to invest in production capacity to meet consumer demand.

Wayfair shares ticked down after the online furniture seller posted a fourth-quarter loss.

Energy drink maker Celsius agreed to buy rival Alani Nu for $1.8 billion, tapping into its popularity with social-media influencers.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: RBA Won't Commit to Any Set Course on Rates
data.description:
By James Glynn

SYDNEY--The Reserve Bank of Australia is not committed to any course of action on interest rates in the coming months, with Governor Michele Bullock telling parliament Friday that easing policy too quickly may stoke a fresh wave of inflationary pressures.

"The board is committed to being guided by the incoming data and our evolving assessment of the risks," Bullock said. "While the strong employment growth is good news for job seekers, we are alert to the possibility that it is signaling a bit more strength in the economy, which could delay or derail the disinflation process."

The comments come after the RBA cut interest rates on Tuesday for the first time since 2020, lowering the official cash rate to 4.10% from 4.35%.

When announcing the cut, Bullock said money market expectations of a string of cuts this year were "far too confident."

"If monetary policy is eased too quickly or by too much, disinflation could stall and inflation would settle above the midpoint of the target range," Bullock said. "So the board remains cautious about prospects for further policy easing."

Write to James Glynn at james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ

data.title: Live Nation to Gain from Beer, Parking Tickets as it Adds Venues — Market Talk
data.description: Live Nation Entertainment's plan to add 20 large venues, two of which will be located in Bogotá and Toronto, through 2026 should help the company add revenue growth. While the world's largest concert promoter is currently making money attractive returns from stadiums shows, it's not counting the beer money and the parking money it would get with its own venues. That would be a key component for Live Nation's financials given the high margins that come from the sale of beer and parking tickets. Live Nation shares fall 1.4% to $150.17 after hours. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

data.title: Investors in Elastic N.V. Should Contact Levi & Korsinsky Before April 14, 2025 to Discuss Your Rights - ESTC
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Elastic N.V. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/elastic-n-v-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=131080&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Elastic N.V. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between May 31, 2024 and August 29, 2024.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) Elastic had implemented significant changes to its sales operations, particularly with respect to its customer segments in the Americas; (ii) the foregoing changes were likely to, and did, disrupt Elastic's sales operations during the first quarter of its FY 2025; (iii) accordingly, defendants had overstated the stability of Elastic's sales operations; (iv) as a result of all the foregoing, Elastic was unlikely to meet its own previously issued revenue guidance for its FY 2025; and (v) as a result, defendants' public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Elastic stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/elastic-n-v-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=131080&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

data.title: Napco Security Technologies, Inc. (NSSC) Shareholders May Have Been Affected by Fraud - Levi & Korsinsky Investigates
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Napco Security Technologies, Inc. ("Napco Security Technologies, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On February 3, 2025, news website Investing.com published an article entitled "NAPCO Security stock tumbles after Q2 sales decline." The article announce a 27 % drop in NAPCO's share price "following the announcement of its fiscal 2025 second quarter results, which revealed a decrease in net sales and diluted earnings per share (EPS) compared to the same period last year." The article further stated that "Richard Soloway, Chairman and CEO, attributed the equipment revenue shortfall to lagging sales in intrusion and access alarm products and door locking devices. The company cited specific issues with two of its larger distributors; one reduced purchases to cut inventory levels, and another underwent a management restructuring that delayed transaction approvals. Soloway expressed disappointment in the overall equipment sales but remains optimistic about future improvements."

On February 3, 2025, NAPCO shares fell over 27% in intraday trading. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/napco-security-technologies-inc-lawsuit-submission-form-2?prid=131083&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. either via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or by telephone at (212)363-7500.

data.title: Materials Down as Investors Brace for Tariffs — Materials Roundup
data.description:
Producers of metals and other raw materials fell as investors braced for the impact of tariffs.

One brokerage warned that a recent copper rally could stall. "U.S. trade policy uncertainty (is) a key global growth headwind: The imposition of 10% tariffs by the U.S. on imports from China, and the threat of tariffs on auto, microchip and metal imports, among others, are creating significant economic uncertainty, which we see as a headwind to global growth this year," said strategists at brokerage BNP Paribas.

Constellium shares climbed after the aluminum supplier said there could be some opportunities from the U.S. tariffs on aluminum. Rio Tinto, the biggest shipper of foreign aluminum into the U.S., has contingency plans ready if President Trump imposes a 25% tariff on imports of the metal, with Europe a potential destination.

Anglo American wrote down the value of its embattled De Beers diamond business by another $2.9 billion as it seeks to ready the division for disposal as part of the miner's efforts to simplify its portfolio.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com
